pages.purpose: Landing page introducing Demeo and navigation hub

important_elements.label: Try the Demo Room link
important_elements.why: Primary call to action to experience the demo room

important_elements.label: Generate My GTM Flow link
important_elements.why: Starts GTM flow generation on dashboard

allowed_actions.label: Navigate to About
allowed_actions.intent: Learn about the company and founders

pages.purpose: Company setup form to provide company details for tailored AI output

important_elements.label: Company Name input
important_elements.why: Primary company identifier for AI context

important_elements.label: One-liner Description textarea
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea[placeholder="We help B2B SaaS teams turn cold outreach into qualified pipeline with AI demo rooms."]
important_elements.why: Brief company description for AI understanding

important_elements.label: Industry selection buttons
important_elements.selector_hint: button:has-text("SaaS"), button:has-text("FinTech"), button:has-text("HealthTech"), button:has-text("EdTech"), button:has-text("E-commerce"), button:has-text("DevTools"), button:has-text("MarTech"), button:has-text("Marketplace"), button:has-text("AI / ML"), button:has-text("Cybersecurity")

pages.purpose: Agent Lab to test and view specialist AI agents

important_elements.label: Content Agent open page link
important_elements.why: Navigate to Content Agent for GTM assets

important_elements.label: Legal Agent open page link
important_elements.why: Navigate to Legal Agent for legal issue scans

pages.purpose: About page describing company mission and founders
Content Agent page to generate GTM assets

allowed_actions.element_id: a[href="/onboarding"]:has-text("No company profile — set up now for better results")

allowed_actions.element_id: a[href="/onboarding"]:has-text("No company profile — set up now for better results")

notes: All navigation actions are based on actual links found on pages.
No destructive or unsafe actions (like form submissions or edits) are included.
Disabled buttons are not included as clickable actions.
Highlight actions are omitted to keep the manifest conservative and focused on navigation.

decisions.question: What does the founder need to provide?
decisions.reply: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

events.say: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

decisions.reason: This is a direct factual question answered by the approved Q&A in the founder input.

decisions.question: Show me the agent/content console.
decisions.reply: Here is the Content Agent page where the founder can generate GTM assets and view AI output based on the company context.

events.say: Opening the Agent Lab page where you can access various AI agents including the Demo Agent.
Navigating to the Content Agent page to show the agent/content console.

decisions.reason: The Content Agent page is the relevant console for generating content and AI output from company context, accessible via the Agents page.

decisions.question: Show the prospect demo room.
decisions.reply: This is the private AI demo room interface where prospects can ask questions and interact with the AI demo agent.

events.say: Showing the Demo Room page where prospects can interact with the AI demo agent.

decisions.reason: The demo page is the prospect-facing AI demo room interface.

decisions.question: Show me what the founder receives after the demo.
decisions.reply: After the demo, the founder receives a CRM summary and follow-up management interface to qualify leads and send follow-ups.

events.say: Showing the CRM summary and follow-up management page that the founder receives after a prospect finishes the demo.

decisions.reason: The CRM page is where the founder views lead qualification and follow-up output after the demo.

decisions.question: What does the founder need to provide?
decisions.reply: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

events.say: Here’s the setup where founders provide their company and product context so the AI demo can tailor its output.
Company/product identifier (e.g., company name and context) to ground the demo.
A one-liner description of what the company does.
Industry/persona selection for more relevant answers and walkthroughs.
In the full founder onboarding, this corresponds to: product/staging URL + any sandbox credentials, target persona, demo goals, walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

decisions.reason: Matches the approved answer and visually ties it to the company setup page.

decisions.question: Should the runtime click the real app directly?
decisions.reply: The MVP should use an approved manifest and visual event playback. Playwright is better for setup-time extraction and validation. Live app control can come later for approved low-risk paths.

events.say: This operator dashboard represents the controlled, manifest-based demo setup—agents and flows are orchestrated without unsafe live tapping.
Generate the GTM flow from approved inputs.
So for the MVP: use approved visual playback + setup-time extraction/validation (e.g., Playwright). Reserve live app control for later, only on explicitly approved low-risk paths.

decisions.reason: Visually anchors the response in the operator dashboard while stating the approved product principle.

decisions.question: Can this support voice?
decisions.reply: Yes. Gemini Live or a LiveKit voice agent can call the same safe demo tools used by text: show page, move cursor, highlight, propose click, answer from knowledge, and update lead memory.

events.say: The agent lab shows the demo is tool-driven—so the same actions can be triggered by text or voice.
Demo agent interface (the brain that guides the prospect through the room).
Yes—voice can use Gemini Live or a LiveKit voice agent, but it still calls the same safe demo tools (navigate/show, cursor movement, highlighting, question answering, and updating lead memory).

decisions.reason: Supports the voice claim by tying it to the tool-based agent architecture shown in the agent lab.

decisions.question: Show me the agent/content console.
decisions.reply: This is the agent lab where GTM/content and demo-focused agents run under the same safe, guided demo tooling.

events.say: Agent Lab (the console behind the personalized demo).
Content Agent — used to generate GTM assets from the founder’s provided context.
If you want, we can jump into the content agent console next to show generation driven by the setup context.

decisions.reason: Uses the closest available console page (Agent Lab) and highlights the Content Agent path.

decisions.question: Show the prospect demo room.
decisions.reply: Here’s the private demo room where the prospect asks questions and the AI guides the guided experience.

events.say: Prospect Demo Room: the AI-guided, personalized experience.
Prospects can ask anything about the product here.
When they’re done, they move to the CRM summary.

decisions.reason: Directly shows the approved demo-room page and highlights the key prospect UI elements.

decisions.question: Show me what the founder receives after the demo.
decisions.reply: After the prospect finishes, Demeo produces a CRM summary and follow-up content the founder can review and send.

events.say: CRM summary + follow-up output generated from the prospect’s demo interactions.
Send follow-up (generated by the system and ready for outreach).
Send now → the founder can deliver the edited/generated email.
This is what the founder receives after the prospect finishes: an organized CRM view plus follow-up content that qualifies the lead.

decisions.reason: Shows the CRM page to represent post-demo deliverables and highlights follow-up actions.
